identity.notes: Read-only endpoint set; requires at least directory read permissions in app registration.

security.notes: Sign-in and directory audit data is typically available for read-only security roles with audit permissions.

conditional_access.notes: Conditional Access policy graph endpoints are policy-scoped and may require policy admin or equivalent directory read roles.

defender.notes: Defender alert/incidence and secure score APIs are covered by Security Reader in most environments.

service_health.notes: Service health and message center data is available in read-only audit scenarios when reporting permissions are granted.

purview.notes: Most collector commands are CLI-driven and require authenticated M365 CLI session plus app-level Purview/eDiscovery permissions.
optional_commands: m365 purview dlp policy list --output json
m365 purview auditlog export create --output json --help

ediscovery.notes: Export and hold operations may be CLI-driven and require additional compliance/eDiscovery tenant permissions.
optional_commands: m365 purview ediscovery case list --output json
m365 purview ediscovery search list --output json
m365 purview ediscovery reviewset list --output json

auth_methods.notes: Authentication method policy and registration reports can require report-reading access.

reports_usage.notes: Global Reader can access tenant-level usage reporting, while detailed report visibility often requires Reports Reader or a workload-specific reader role.

external_identity.notes: External identity policy inventory covers cross-tenant access, invitation policy, and authentication flow posture.

consent_policy.notes: Consent policy inventory covers admin consent workflow and permission grant policies.

domains_hybrid.notes: Hybrid posture is inferred from domains and sampled on-premises sync markers on users.

intune.notes: Will return empty/unavailable datasets if tenant lacks Intune licensing.

sharepoint.notes: SharePoint tenant settings and site inventory can vary by role and service readiness.

sharepoint_access.notes: Sampled site permissions are attempted in a reader-first mode, but Graph site permission depth can require stronger SharePoint privileges or app-only escalation.

onedrive_posture.notes: OneDrive posture uses SharePoint read APIs and site URL heuristics; admin-center-only OneDrive details may still be unavailable to Global Reader.

teams.notes: Teams endpoints are still readable in many read-only role patterns.

teams_policy.notes: Teams policy depth uses PowerShell-backed cmdlets and usually needs Teams PowerShell tooling and tenant admin connectivity.

app_consent.notes: Enterprise application consent depth depends on delegated grant and app role assignment visibility.

licensing.notes: License assignment details are sampled and can be cross-correlated with SKU inventory.

identity_governance.notes: Governance and PIM schedule visibility depends on Entra ID Governance licensing and role-scoped APIs.

intune_depth.notes: Deeper Intune policy and assignment surfaces can still return empty or partial results when Intune is not licensed or the tenant does not use those workloads.

exchange.notes: Command-based Exchange posture relies on Microsoft 365 CLI tenant and report commands; mailbox presence falls back to Graph only when CLI reporting is unavailable.
optional_commands: m365 outlook report mailboxusagemailboxcount --period D30 --output json
m365 outlook report mailboxusagequotastatusmailboxcounts --period D30 --output json
m365 outlook report mailboxusagestorage --period D30 --output json
m365 outlook report mailactivitycounts --period D30 --output json

exchange_policy.notes: Exchange policy depth uses Exchange PowerShell cmdlets and requires PowerShell plus Exchange connectivity.
